name: A Perfectly Normal Walk
Description: There's rumors in Ars Delusia that sometimes, when one walks isolated streets all alone, they can hear the {i}click-clack{/i} of someone's high heels chasing after them, the sound getting irresistibly closer and closer until it is too late...

theScene: As you traverse the Ars Delusian streets, you abruptly and unexplainably find your daytime urban stroll plunged into the eerie dark of the night, as if someone had simply and purely turned off the sun, the baffling change leaving your way lit up only by the surrounding buildings, and making the atmosphere of your walk a suddenly much tenser one...

Continuing to stride down the urban road on your lonesome, you find nary a split, crossing, or alleyway in sight as you look around, the wide concrete pathway ahead of and behind you somehow appearing to stretch on and on into the dark without end.

With nevertheless little choice but to carry on wherever your path may lead, you move onwards, step by step, the sounds of your own footsteps the only thing to keep you company through the seemingly interminable night.

Slowly, an eerie stillness settles over you as minute after minute passes and you still fail to see anything around you but the indifferent buildings that loom forebodingly over you and barely light your way with their stark windows and cold neon signs, leaving you feeling increasingly unnerved by how you don't appear to be going anywhere, not to mention how devoid of people or sound everything seems to be.
But just as you start feeling physically and mentally fatigued from all this aimless wandering...
The sound of heels clicking on the pavement suddenly starts echoing behind you, sending a chill up your spine.
Move to the side and let whoever this is pass.

In fact, you realize that the footsteps are now {i}right behind you{/i}, your own feet suddenly stopping against your will as a cold chill grips your very core, meanwhile whoever is following you also halts their heel-clicking steps, plunging the street back into total silence.

Except now, you can {b}feel{/b} the oppressive presence behind you towering over you like a lead weight while dark flickers ominously dance along the edges of your vision, like {i}they{/i} are peeking around at you with curious intent.

You look behind you to see who is there.

The only thing you see is the vacant urban vista you just walked past.

There is no one else here.
Then just as you turn back, the sound of heels clicking against the pavement resumes, and continues to draw closer.

EventText.NameOfScene: Move to the side and let whoever this is pass.
theScene: You move against one of the buildings that border the sidewalk in order to let the one behind you pass, a nerve-wracking, heart-racing tension tightening through your entire body as the sound of the heeled footsteps gets closer and closer, up to the point where it feels like whoever is making them is passing {i}right by you{/i}...

It feels like the lights are getting dimmer.
The sound of footsteps slowly keeps growing closer to you while a tingling chill continues to creep over your body.

The tingling chill continues to creep along your skin, turning into a cold tremor.
Everything keeps getting darker and darker, the road ahead seeming to stretch on and on into a pitch-black eternity.

Closer and closer, until with one decisive {i}clack{/i}, that chilling presence catches up to you!

The creeping, stilling chill vanishes as your foot hits the ground.
The sound of heels is gone, the resulting silence feeling downright deafening, but also impossibly liberating at the same time, like you've just been freed from the influence of... something.
Deciding to stand your ground, you stop trudging forward... but the sound of clicking heels does not.
In what feels like the passing of a single heartbeat, as if all it had waited for was for you to halt, the {i}click-clack{/i} sound catches up to you, and stops behind you!
Whoever or whatever those footsteps belong to is now standing right at your back, and you can {b}feel{/b} their towering presence looming over you, their unseen gaze tangibly boring into you.

In a mad dash, you start to sprint down the street to get away from whoever is behind you... But to your increased fear, you can hear the gaps between their heel-clicking footfalls somehow lengthen rather than speed up as the looming presence seemingly works to keep pace with you!

As you keep rushing down the concrete street at all the speed that your legs can muster, the sound of the clicking heels slowly but surely grows ever more distant, until finally, it fades entirely, meanwhile that odd stillness that pervaded the air vanishes completely, its sudden absence bringing with it an incredible feeling of liberation that makes you feel mercifully alone and unfollowed once more.
Halting your run, you take just a moment to catch your breath and get your bearings, then you straighten back up and decide you shouldn't tarry, lest your strange pursuer somehow return.

No matter how fast you run, the footsteps seem to always get closer, meanwhile the concrete road ahead never seems to end, the nighttime darkness ever yawning before you, making for a foreboding, demoralizing sight that swiftly wears away at both your hope and endurance...

Until you're eventually forced to stop and catch your breath, heaving exhaustion and instinctive panic pounding through every fiber of your being alongside the beating of your strained heart as you stand doubled over with your hands braced on your shaky legs.
